點樣搞到老婆都係bc

bc既配偶可以有配偶簽證，住滿3年有永居，1年後可以申請bc
如果係居英權計劃既bc，可以傳一代比個仔，其後就要係英國國內出生先可以有bc
非居英權計劃既bc，佢會按返你既上5代既國籍狀況，容許你子孫邊度出世都可以傳bc

巴打你check下好wor
http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1990/34/schedule/2
居英權計劃主申請人憑第 1(1)段取得BC的人士先係 "BC otherwise than by descent" "非憑世系取得的BC" 
配偶和未成年子女憑附表2取得BC的人士係 "BC by descent" "憑世系取得的BC" 

唔信可以check 
張certificate 右下角日期之下 "Section" 一欄 
主申請人（你爸）應該係"1(1)" 
你應該唔係 （你張certificate "Section"係寫乜?) 

你透過父母攞，即係你當年係未成年子女 
你的BC係憑世系取得 
你仔女一定要係英國出世先有BC 

不過補充一句，如果你真係飛去英國生仔 
根據中國國籍法你子女將不是中國國籍 
可以拿香港身份證 
但係唔會有回鄉證或特區護照 
每一次去大陸要拿中國簽證 
如果要加人中國國籍的話就要放棄BC 

Ref: http://forum3.hkgolden.com/view.aspx?message=6250186&page=6

Have you got a valid British Citizen passport? If not, apply for one now using the link below -

https://www.gov.uk/overseas-passports

You will need to register with the NMC (Nursing and Midwifery Council) in order to practice as a nurse in the UK. Please see the link below for details -

https://www.nmc.org.uk/registration/joining-the-register/trained-outside-the-eueea/

Once you are settle in the UK, you will need to apply for a National Insurance number (NI number). Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

You should have a look at the website below. It list all the NHS nursing jobs in Scotland -

https://jobs.scot.nhs.uk/_results.aspx?catID=5&regionID=&orgID=&word=

有咩辦法搞到個仔都係bc

1. 英國出世
2. 英國以外地方出世: 你要係佢出世前連續係英國住滿3年, 其中唔可以以多過270日離開英國


2. 英國以外地方出世: 你要係佢出世前連續係英國住滿3年, 其中唔可以以多過270日離開英國

The above is section 3(2) registration and it does not suit most people who are British Citizen by descent under the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990. If you look at sections 3(2) and 3(3) -

(2)A person born outside the United Kingdom [F26and the qualifying territories] shall be entitled, on an application for his registration as a British citizen made [F27while he is a minor], to be registered as such a citizen if the requirements specified in subsection (3) or, in the case of a person born stateless, the requirements specified in paragraphs (a) and (b) of that subsection, are fulfilled in the case of either that person’s father or his mother (“the parent in question”).

(3)The requirements referred to in subsection (2) are—

(a)that the parent in question was a British citizen by descent at the time of the birth; and

(b)that the father or mother of the parent in question—

(i)was a British citizen otherwise than by descent at the time of the birth of the parent in question; or

(ii)became a British citizen otherwise than by descent at commencement, or would have become such a citizen otherwise than by descent at commencement but for his or her death;

For those of you who received British Citizenship via the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 as a dependent of the main applicant, you cannot get section 3(2) registration for your overseas born child because your parents do not fulfill section 3(3)(b) - highlighted in red.

Apply for an UK spouse visa. The whole family will need to move to the UK. There are a number of requirements for an UK spouse visa -

1) housing in the UK cannot be overcrowded

2) financial requirement - either cash savings worth £62,500 for at lease six months or the British Citizen working in the UK for six months of more with a salary of at lease £18,600 per year. The financial requirement will be higher if you have non British Citizen children.

3) Relationship - the relationship with the British Citizen needs to be genuine

4) English language

You will need to apply for the UK spouse visa in Hong Kong. The spouse will be given a visa for 2.5 years and need to meet the above requirements again when renewing the UK spouse visa. The spouse can get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) after 5 years and can apply for British Citizenship after getting ILR if still married to a British Citizen.

我老豆傳左本BC比我，條女無BC，第時生仔都唔知點搞


The only sure way to get British Citizenship for your child is to have the birth in the UK.

One more thing - some of you may wish to consider giving birth in Northern Ireland (Belfast for example). This is because Irish nationality law states that any child born in the Island of Ireland with at lease one British Citizen parent will also get Irish citizenship. Having Irish citizenship means that the child will continue to enjoy EU freedom of movement after Brexit.
